rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 10 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that the command-line argument can exceed the 256-byte buffer, causing a stack buffer overflow. This localizes the unsafe copy and ties it to attacker-controlled argv data size.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy/snprintf but did not include rejecting overlong input before copying. Thus the fix lacks the required explicit length check with rejection in addition to null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended banning unsafe string functions and using bounded alternatives and a length-carrying string wrapper type, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
